title: goodenia connata
intro: Goodenia connata is a perennial herb native to the southwestern region of Western Australia, commonly known as the 'native violet'. It has been traditionally used in Western Australian Aboriginal medicine, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been found to have antioxidant and antimicrobial effects, providing health benefits such as wound healing and anti-inflammatory responses. Goodenia connata can be prepared as a tincture, infusion, or poultice for its medicinal and therapeutic uses.
